tone.overall: Casual, conversational, and highly approachable — like a Twitch streamer teaching a college class
tone.humor: Frequent dry humor, self-deprecating jokes, and playful teasing of students and the subject matter
tone.energy: High energy, enthusiastic, occasionally irreverent — keeps things entertaining
tone.attitude_toward_students: Friendly and encouraging, treats students as peers, uses 'bro', 'dude', 'y'all' naturally

pacing.speed: Fast-talking with frequent short pauses mid-thought
pacing.sentence_length: Short, punchy sentences — rarely more than 15 words before a pause or pivot
pacing.rhythm: Stream-of-consciousness delivery with lots of self-interruptions and tangents that loop back to the main point
pacing.transitions: Abrupt topic shifts marked by 'Okay, so...', 'Alright, so...', 'Now...'

framing_style.explanation_approach: Uses vivid real-world analogies and hypothetical scenarios to introduce concepts — e.g., Yale admissions processing 10,000 applications, or asking 'Wouldn't it be cool if...'
framing_style.audience_engagement: Constantly asks rhetorical questions ('Right?', 'Wouldn't that be cool?', 'Am I right?') and invites chat participation
framing_style.simplification: Deliberately avoids jargon, translates technical terms into plain language ('embedding means turn your text into a bunch of numbers'), admits when he doesn't understand something ('I don't understand tokens, but I get text')
framing_style.storytelling: Opens with anecdotes (Twitter stories, personal experiences) before diving into the topic, frequently references pop culture (Harry Potter, Twitch streaming)
framing_style.authority_style: Positions himself as a peer learning alongside students rather than lecturing from above — 'Hell no, I just told the cursor to do it'

verbal_quirks: Addresses the AI as 'she/her' — 'I asked her, like, yo, how big is your context window'
Uses 'yo' as a casual address to both students and AI
Says 'crashed out' and other Gen-Z slang naturally
Frequently says 'right?' at the end of statements seeking confirmation
Drops in 'like and subscribe' as a running joke about the livestream format
Often trails off with '...' before picking up a new thought

lecture_structure.opening: Starts with off-topic news/anecdotes and class logistics before the main content
lecture_structure.teaching_pattern: Pose a problem → ask why it matters → introduce the solution concept → show how it works with a concrete example → show the code
lecture_structure.closing_moves: Summarizes with casual language, reinforces key takeaways, reassures students not to stress
